# Index by Conjecture

**Source**: 60 papers (P001-P060)

## C1 -- Insuffisance de δ¹ (Instruction-level defenses are insufficient alone)

Papers providing evidence that system prompts and instruction-level protections cannot prevent prompt injection.

| ID | Title | Support | Evidence |
|----|-------|---------|----------|
| P001 | HouYi (Liu et al.) | **Direct** | 86.1% of apps vulnerable despite system prompts |
| P003 | Comprehensive Review | **Direct** | Taxonomy confirms multi-layer vulnerabilities |
| P004 | WASP Benchmark | **Direct** | Web agents with system prompts fail |
| P006 | ToolHijacker | **Direct** | System prompts cannot prevent tool manipulation |
| P010 | Protocol Exploits | **Direct** | Prompt-level defenses bypassed via protocol attacks |
| P018 | Shallow Alignment | **Indirect** | RLHF concentrates on early tokens only |
| P023 | SSRA / Safety Misalignment | **Indirect** | Four strategies bypass alignment |
| P024 | Separation Score | **Direct** | Inconsistent separation across models |
| P026 | IPI in the Wild | **Direct** | System prompts fail against indirect injection |
| P027 | Medical Framework | **Indirect** | Prompt-level guardrails evaluated as insufficient |
| P028 | Safe AI Clinicians | **Direct** | Role-playing exploits system prompt weaknesses |
| P029 | JAMA Medical Injection | **Direct** | Commercial prompt guardrails bypassed at 94.4% |
| P030 | Declining Safety Messaging | **Direct** | System prompts no longer enforce disclaimers |
| P033 | Self-Policing (HiddenLayer) | **Direct** | LLM judge prompts share same vulnerabilities |
| P035 | MPIB Medical Benchmark | **Direct** | Direct injections target system prompt layer |
| P036 | LRM Autonomous Jailbreak | **Direct** | Multi-turn attacks erode system prompt protections |
| P037 | Jailbreaking Survey | **Direct** | Perception layer covers prompt obfuscation |
| P038 | InstruCoT Defense | **Indirect** | Enhanced post-training needed beyond system prompts |
| P039 | GRP-Obliteration | **Indirect** | Single prompt erases alignment |
| P040 | Healthcare Misinformation | **Direct** | Direct injections bypass system prompt in medical context |
| P045 | System Prompt Poisoning | **Direct** | System prompt itself is the attack vector |
| P047 | Defense by Leveraging Attack | **Indirect** | Confirms δ¹ alone insufficient; needs attack-aware defense |
| P048 | SLR on PI Defenses | **Direct** | Taxonomy shows instruction-level is one of many necessary layers |
| P050 | JMedEthicBench | **Direct** | Multi-turn degradation erodes instruction compliance |
| P052 | RLHF Alignment Shallow | **Indirect** | Early-token concentration means instructions fade |
| P053 | Semantic Jailbreaks | **Direct** | Semantic attacks bypass instruction-level defenses |
| P056 | AIR -- Instruction Hierarchy | **Indirect** | Input-layer-only IH signals insufficient; intermediate layers needed |
| P057 | ASIDE -- Architectural Separation | **Direct** | Separation achievable architecturally, proving instruction-level alone fails |
| P059 | In-Paper PI | **Partial** | Static/iterative attacks defeat instruction-following |
| P060 | SoK: Evaluating Guardrails | **Direct** | No single guardrail is universal |

**Total C1 support**: 30 papers (22 direct, 6 indirect, 2 partial)

## C2 -- Necessite de δ³ (Formal verification / external enforcement is necessary)
